text.title: Agent Failure Taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-failure-taxonomy enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every observed instance gets exactly one primary label from the taxonomy. Secondary tags are allowed and tracked separately.
rule.rationale: Multi-primary labels destroy aggregation. Dashboards over-count, postmortems get muddled.

rule.statement: A label is added to the taxonomy only after ≥3 instances justify it AND the owner approves. No ad-hoc labels.
rule.rationale: Unconstrained label creation = no labels. Every team-member spins their own variant.

rule.statement: Re-classifications preserve history: the prior label and timestamp stay on record. Label rewrites are append-only, not destructive.
rule.rationale: Destructive re-tagging makes trend lines meaningless. Append-only preserves the learning that drove the re-label.

rule.statement: Each label maps to a defined remediation lane. A label that does not change action is decoration, not classification.
rule.rationale: Labels exist to route response. If 'hallucination' and 'tool-misuse' lead to the same fix, merge them.

rule.statement: Default 6 labels: `hallucination`, `reasoning-shortfall`, `tool-misuse`, `context-overflow`, `prompt-injection`, `drift`. Teams may extend per r2.
rule.rationale: Six covers ≥90% of observed incidents in 2026 data; teams can add but should not start from zero.
